title: Notes from the field: rotavirus vaccine administration errors--United States, 2006-2013.
ehr_evidence: The study used reports from the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS).
summary: Analysis of rotavirus vaccine administration errors, specifically injection errors and eye splashes, in the United States between 2006 and 2013.
key_findings: Common reasons for errors included misinterpreting package inserts, confusing syringes/vials, and inadequate training
population: Infants and children in the United States
limitations: Passive surveillance system may capture only a small fraction of errors
